BENEOS.Settings.PatreonLogin.Name: Login to Beneos Cloud
BENEOS.Settings.PatreonLogin.Label: Login to Beneos Cloud
BENEOS.Settings.PatreonLogin.Hint: Connect your Patreon account so Moulinette can deliver your content.
BENEOS.Settings.CloudDisconnect.Name: Disconnect from Beneos Cloud
BENEOS.Settings.CloudDisconnect.Label: Disconnect from Beneos Cloud
BENEOS.Settings.CloudDisconnect.Hint: Sign out of Beneos Cloud on this world.
BENEOS.Settings.CloudAccount.Name: Beneos Cloud Account
BENEOS.Settings.CloudAccount.Label: Manage Account
BENEOS.Settings.CloudAccount.Hint: Sign in to Beneos Cloud or disconnect the active session. The button adapts to the current login state.
BENEOS.Settings.CleanCompendium.Name: Empty Compendium
BENEOS.Settings.CleanCompendium.Label: Reset Beneos Module Compendiums
BENEOS.Settings.CleanCompendium.Hint: Removes all entries from Beneos compendiums and resets token configurations. Installed content will need to be re-imported.
BENEOS.Settings.SearchEngine.Name: Beneos Cloud
BENEOS.Settings.SearchEngine.Label: Find and Install Beneos Content
BENEOS.Settings.SearchEngine.Hint: Open the Beneos search engine to browse and install tokens, battlemaps, items and spells.
BENEOS.Settings.DeathManagement.Name: Automatic Death Icon
BENEOS.Settings.DeathManagement.Hint: Creatures that reach 0 HP automatically switch to a death icon.
BENEOS.Settings.SetupTour.Name: Beneos Setup Tour
BENEOS.Settings.SetupTour.Label: Start Setup Tour
BENEOS.Settings.SetupTour.Hint: Guides you through Moulinette, authentication and content installation.
BENEOS.Settings.ShowNav.Name: Show DM Navigation to Players
BENEOS.Settings.ShowNav.Hint: Makes Beneos navigation tiles and map note icons visible to players. Usually best left disabled - these elements are intended for the GM.
BENEOS.Tour.PostInstall.Title: Start Beneos Tutorial?
BENEOS.Tour.PostInstall.Content:
The Beneos Getting Started content has been installed.

Would you like to start the Beneos Tutorial now? It walks you through every feature of the module on a guided sequence of demo scenes.

If you say No, you can launch it any time from Settings → Module Settings → Beneos.
BENEOS.Tour.FirstRun.Title: Beneos Tour
BENEOS.Tour.FirstRun.Content: A module update or first install has been detected. If you're new to Beneos or haven't seen our tour yet, we recommend starting it - our tours showcase our products and the unique, seamless integration that only Beneos has to offer.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.Title: Beneos Search Engine Tour
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.Desc: Shows you the Beneos Search Engine - an alternative way to browse and install Beneos content by biome, campaign and more.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoIntro.Title: Beneos Search Engine
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoIntro.Content:
Besides Moulinette, there's a second way to browse and install Beneos content: the Beneos Search Engine.

It lets you search by biome, campaign, creature type and more - perfect for finding exactly the right content for your adventure.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoActorsTab.Title: Actor Directory
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoActorsTab.Content:
The Beneos Search Engine lives in the Actor Directory in the sidebar.

We've opened the Actors tab for you.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoActorsButton.Title: Beneos Cloud Button
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoActorsButton.Conten:
Below "Create Actor" and "Create Folder" you can see the large "Beneos Cloud - Search & Download" button.

This opens the Beneos Search Engine. Let's open it now.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoBeneosCloud.Title: Beneos Tokens, Spells & Items
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoBeneosCloud.Content:
The Beneos Search Engine is now open. By default it starts on the Tokens tab.

Beneos Tokens, Spells & Items is our second Patreon - animated creatures, equipment and spell effects.

The login section at the bottom is only for Beneos Tokens, Spells & Items subscribers. If you're a Beneos Battle Maps patron, you don't need to log in here.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoSearchOverview.Cont:
The Search Engine has four tabs at the top:

  • Tokens - Animated creatures and NPCs (Beneos Tokens Patreon)
  • Maps - Battlemaps and sceneries (Beneos Battle Maps Patreon)
  • Items - Equipment and gear (Beneos Tokens Patreon)
  • Spells - Animated spell effects (Beneos Tokens Patreon)

As a Beneos Battle Maps patron, the Maps tab is most relevant for you.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoMapsTab.Title: Maps Tab
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoMapsTab.Content:
We've switched to the Maps tab for you.

Here you can browse all available Beneos Battlemaps - from dungeons and taverns to forests and mountains.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoMapsFilters.Title: Filtering by Biome & Campaign
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoMapsFilters.Content:
Use the filter dropdowns to narrow your search:

  • Biome - Location type (Forest, Desert, Cave, etc.)
  • Campaign - Adventure or campaign
  • Type - Sceneries vs. Battlemaps
  • Size - Map dimensions

Combine multiple filters to find the perfect map for your session.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoMapsDownload.Title: Download with Moulinette
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoMapsDownload.Conten:
Found the perfect map? Click the download icon on any map to open it directly in the Moulinette Browser.

From there you can import the scene with all its assets - lighting, walls, sounds and effects included.
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoComplete.Title: You're All Set!
BENEOS.Tour.Demo.DemoComplete.Content:
You now know both ways to find and install Beneos content:

  • Moulinette Browser - Direct browsing by creator and pack
  • Beneos Search Engine - Search by biome, campaign, creature type and more

Use whichever works best for you - or combine both.

Have fun playing! For questions visit us on Discord or Patreon.
BENEOS.Tour.StartHere.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Start Here
BENEOS.Tour.StartHere.StartWelcome.Title: Beneos Tour
BENEOS.Tour.StartHere.StartWelcome.Conte: Your tour starts in 10 seconds
BENEOS.Tour.StartHere.StartExplainer.Con:
This is the Beneos Getting Started Tour.

We'll walk you through every aspect of both Beneos Patreons:

  • Beneos Battle Maps
  • Beneos Tokens, Spells & Items

Use the Next and Previous buttons at the bottom to move through the tour at your own pace.
BENEOS.Tour.StartHere.StartBattlemapsInf:
Beneos Battle Maps offers a complete library for an immersive gameplay experience:

  • 2500+ scenes
  • Intro sequences
  • Sceneries
  • Audio files
  • Handouts

Everything pre-configured and ready to use.
BENEOS.Tour.StartHere.StartTokensInfo.Co:
Beneos Tokens, Spells & Items is our second Patreon, focused on creatures and effects:

  • 400+ official SRD creatures
  • 300+ custom-designed creatures - growing every week
  • A large archive of custom spells and items

All features will be explained later in this tutorial.
BENEOS.Tour.StartHere.StartContinue.Cont:
Now we'll continue with Beneos Battle Maps.

The tour continues in 5 seconds.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Overview Map
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.Desc: Learn what an Overview Map is and how the Beneos navigation works.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Welcome.Title: Overview Map
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Welcome.Content:
This is an Overview Map. Because we offer more content than fits on a single map, every Beneos release ships with an Overview like this.

This is the Ashur Fire Temple. Take a moment to look around.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1PinNotice.Title: Something's Missing
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1PinNotice.Content:
Notice anything? The overview looks very empty.

That's because the navigation icons need to be activated first. Many users forget this step and wonder why their map looks blank.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1PinActivate.Title: Activate Journal Pins
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1PinActivate.Content:
You can activate the icons in the Journal area of the left toolbar (highlighted). Click the Journal button, then press the third button from the top - the Pin toggle.

Click Next and we'll activate them for you.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1PinActivated.Title: Icons Activated
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1PinActivated.Content:
All navigation icons are now visible on the map.

Let's explore what they do.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Teleporters.Title: DM Teleporters
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Teleporters.Content:
These are DM Teleporters. Only you as the DM can see them - they're how you navigate between scenes of a release.

The door icon takes you to the Battlemap (the tactical view). The image icon takes you to the Scenery (an immersive camera angle of the same place).

Battlemaps and Sceneries always come as a pair.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1NavbarIntro.Title: The Navigation Bar
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1NavbarIntro.Content:
This is the navigation bar. Every scene has one, and only the DM can see it.

The icons are always the same and always mean the same things. Once you understand one map, you understand them all.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1IntroIcon.Title: Intro Sequences - A Cinematic Opener
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1IntroIcon.Content:
This icon takes you to the release's intro sequence - a short cinematic you can play before the party enters the location.

We'll cover intro sequences in their own chapter later. For now, just know they're one click away from every Overview.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Regional.Title: Region Map & World Map - Zoom Out
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Regional.Content:
The Overview you're looking at is one environment. That environment can sit inside a larger Region - think Barovia in Curse of Strahd, or Avernus in Descent into Avernus. And regions can live inside a full World Map.

These teleporters give you one-click access to every hierarchy level. More on this in the World Map chapter.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Lore.Title: Learn Something About This Place
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Lore.Content:
Every release ships with a lore document - background, story seeds, and lore snippets written for this specific pack.

It doubles as your GM notebook: overwrite or extend it with your own notes, so everything about this environment stays in one place.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1LoreJournal.Title: Background Lore
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1LoreJournal.Content:
This journal contains background lore for the Ashur Fire Temple.

Think of it as inspiration. Every DM should integrate Beneos content into their own world - this is how we use it, but it's also a space for your notes.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Help.Title: Help & Documentation
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Help.Content:
This icon opens the Beneos documentation - all features explained in text, plus deep dives into every module we use.

You'll also find links to our Discord and a FAQ here.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1HelpJournal.Title: Beneos Documentation
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1HelpJournal.Content:
Detailed explanations of all modules, deep dives into features, and usage tips.

This documentation comes with every Beneos release. You can always find it in the Beneos folder in the Journal Explorer.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Compass.Title: Compass - Never Lose Your Bearings
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Compass.Content:
Every Beneos Battlemap and Scenery has a compass in the navbar showing cardinal orientation.

Especially useful on Sceneries - you're viewing the world from an in-scene perspective, and the compass tells you which way you're actually looking.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Summary.Title: Overview Complete
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Summary.Content: That covers the Overview. Let's look at a Battlemap next.
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Continue.Title: DM Teleporters
BENEOS.Tour.Page1.P1Continue.Content:
DMs use the DM Teleporters to travel between scenes. Let's enter the Oracle's battlemap now.

Right-clicking any Beneos teleporter opens this context menu with three options:

  • Activate Scene: pulls you and all connected players onto the linked scene.
  • View Scene: opens the scene only for you, so you can prepare or sneak a peek.
  • Preload Scene: has your players' clients download the scene in the background. Perfect for trimming load times when playing online.

Click Next and we'll take you into the Oracle battlemap.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Battlemap
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.Desc: Learn what a Battlemap is and how to use its navigation and handouts.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmWelcome.Title: This is a Battlemap
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmWelcome.Content:
Battlemaps are your combat and encounter view - the tactical counterpart to a Scenery.

Let's see what players and the DM can do here.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmNavbarIntro.Title: Battlemap Navigation
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmNavbarIntro.Content: The navigation looks almost like what you've seen on the Overview. Battlemaps have a few extra options - let's go through them.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmOverviewTeleporter.C:
This teleporter takes you back to the Overview Map we just came from.

Jump back and forth anytime, even mid-session.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmRegionWorld.Title: Jump Up to Region and World
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmRegionWorld.Content:
These two icons are your zoom-out - one click to the Region Map, another to the full World Map.

They sit in the same spot on every Beneos scene. We'll walk through the World Map in its own chapter.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmSceneryLink.Title: Scenery Link
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmSceneryLink.Content:
Every Battlemap leads to its sister Scenery.

Note: In every release, one Scenery doubles as the Overview Map itself. That Battlemap doesn't have its own sister Scenery, so this icon is greyed out.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmHandouts.Title: Player Handouts
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmHandouts.Content: Most Battlemaps ship with Handouts. They are listed here.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmHandout1.Title: Player Handout
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmHandout1.Content:
Handouts are immersive images from the Battlemap. They serve two purposes:

  • Give you as the DM a feel for the environment and help you describe it.
  • Act as points of interest on the map if they're relevant to your story.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmHandout2.Title: Share Handouts with Players
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmHandout2.Content:
Every handout can be shared with your players in one click. The image pops up on their screens exactly like a physical handout landing on the table - framed, focused, and ready to discuss.

A small feature with a big impact on immersion.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmShareImage.Title: Show to Players
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmShareImage.Content: Click the three-dots menu in the top-right of the handout header and choose Show to Players.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmNavigationRecap.Cont:
You've seen how the Game Master moves between scenes and works with the navigation bar - teleporters, world regions, sceneries, handouts.

Next: how your players move their characters across the maps.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPlayerNavigators.Con: Beneos offers a linked, interactive world. If you want, your players can wander from map to map on their own via Player Teleporters.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPlayerIcon.Title: Player Teleporter
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPlayerIcon.Content: This icon is visible to your players. It marks an interactive teleporter they can use themselves.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPlayerField.Title: Player Teleporter
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPlayerField.Content: See this invisible field on the map? When a player moves their token onto it, a teleport option appears.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPlayerFieldMoved.Con: The player has moved into the teleporter field. They can click the button themselves - or you as the DM can click it - and the token teleports wherever the path leads.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmFollowPlayers.Title: Keep an Eye on Your Players
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmFollowPlayers.Conten:
When a player uses a teleporter, the system sends you a private whisper - only the Game Master sees it. It names the player and their destination.

Click the View Scene link inside that whisper to jump straight to where the player ended up.
This Is Where the Player Landed

This is the exact spot the teleporter dropped the knight.

A teleporter can lead inside the same map (e.g. a different floor) or to a completely different map. Either way, you follow the player via the View Scene link in the DM whisper.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPauseMlt.Title: Player Navigation Can Be Disabled
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPauseMlt.Content:
If you prefer to use each map individually - without a connected world - you can disable all player navigation.

This hides all teleporter icons for players. Let's see where to find that setting.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmPauseMltSettings.Con:
Open Game Settings → Configure Settings in the right sidebar, then find the Multilevel Tokens, by Beneos module.

Check Pause MLT at the top - that's it. Player teleporters and their icons disappear for everyone.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmOverlayTiles.Title: Next: Overlay Tiles
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmOverlayTiles.Content: Many maps have perspective effects that increase immersion: obstacles that characters can walk behind or through.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmObstacles.Title: Depth Through Foreground Tiles
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmObstacles.Content:
Every Beneos battlemap uses foreground layer tiles to add depth and immersion. Walls, pillars, tree canopies - creatures moving past them slip underneath, just like in real life.

But sometimes a character wants to be on top - climbing a wall, scaling a rooftop, or flying overhead. Beneos handles that too.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmForegroundTiles.Titl: Bring a Token to the Foreground
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmForegroundTiles.Cont:
Right-click a player token and enter a height of 20 or more in the elevation field (top-left of the token HUD).

The token now appears above all foreground tiles. Perfect for climbing, flying, or any ability that lifts a character above obstacles.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmComplete.Title: That's Everything About Battlemaps
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmComplete.Content:
You've now seen everything a Battlemap has to offer - the heart of every Beneos release. Teleporters, handouts, tokens, foreground tiles, MLT pausing: it's all in your hands.

This was the biggest chapter. From here on, it gets shorter - I promise.
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmSceneryNext.Title: Next: Sceneries
BENEOS.Tour.Page2.BmSceneryNext.Content: We're jumping to the Scenery of this battlemap now.
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Sceneries
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.Desc: Learn what a Scenery is and how it relates to its Battlemap.
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScWelcome.Title: Sceneries
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScWelcome.Content:
This is a Scenery - the narrative counterpart to a Battlemap. Same location, more atmospheric angle.

Every Battlemap has its own Scenery, and every Scenery links back to its Battlemap.
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScWelcome2.Title: When to Use Sceneries
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScWelcome2.Content:
Use Sceneries when the focus is on roleplay, exploration or atmosphere. When combat starts, jump to the linked Battlemap.

Switching between the two lets you flow naturally between cinematic moments and tactical encounters.
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScNavbar.Title: Navigation
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScNavbar.Content: The Scenery navigation works the same way as the Battlemap navigation you've already seen. Nothing new here.
Battlemaps and Sceneries always come as a pair. The highlighted icon takes you back to the Battlemap that belongs to this Scenery - just like the Scenery icon on the Battlemap brought you here.

Tip: Right-click any interactive navigator to open it with both View Scene and Activate Scene options.
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScStaticMaps.Title: Static Map Variant
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScStaticMaps.Content:
Every animated Battlemap and Scenery has a static version you can switch to if video performance is an issue.

Right-click on any scene - in the navigation bar or in the Scene Directory - and select Use Static Map.

Switch back the same way.
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScStaticFreeze.Title: This Is How Static Looks
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScStaticFreeze.Content:
The scene behind this box is frozen - a preview of how the static variant looks.

No animations, no video decoding, just a clean still image. Click Next to bring the Scenery back to life.
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScNextIntro.Title: Next: Intro Sequences
BENEOS.Tour.Page3.ScNextIntro.Content:
That's all there is to Sceneries.

Next: Intro Sequences - short cinematic moments to impress your players.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Intro Sequences
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.Desc: Learn how to use Beneos Intro Sequences to set the mood for your sessions.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroWelcome.Title: Intro Sequences
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroWelcome.Content: Intro Sequences are short cinematic videos you can play at any moment to immerse your players in the world.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroStartButton.Title: Start and Stop Intros
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroStartButton.Conte: This icon in the navigation bar lets you start and stop the intro. One click plays it, another stops it.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroTokenControl.Cont:
For the navigation buttons to respond, the Token Controls tool must be active in the left toolbar.

Small technical requirement - the navbar lives on the token layer.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroPlay.Title: Impress Your Players
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroPlay.Content: The intro sequence is playing. Use these to set the mood, introduce a new location, or punctuate a key story beat.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroUseFreely.Title: An Offer, Not a Script
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroUseFreely.Content:
Every intro is an offer - footage you can use however you like. Open a new scene with it, use it as a dream sequence, a flashback, a vision, or a transition between chapters.

Whatever fits the moment at your table is the right call.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroManualStart.Title: A Manual Fallback
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroManualStart.Conte:
The navbar button is the easy way to start an intro - but not your only option. You can also start the video manually from the tile itself.

Handy if a module conflict ever keeps the navbar button from firing.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroTileControls.Cont: To trigger an intro manually, switch to Tile Controls in the left toolbar. This activates the tile layer where the intro video lives.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroRightClickTile.Co: Right-click the intro video tile to open its Tile HUD. The HUD has a manual start/stop button for the video.
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroComplete.Title: Intros Covered
BENEOS.Tour.Page4.IntroComplete.Content:
That's everything about Intro Sequences. Use them, skip them, or mix and match - it's your table.

Next: how to build an entire connected world with Beneos content.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.Title: Beneos Tutorial - World Map
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.Desc: Showcase of how a connected Beneos world can look - using the Escalia world map as an example.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmWelcome.Title: The World Map
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmWelcome.Content:
This is the World Map - the highest level of the Beneos hierarchy.

From here, every region, scenery and battlemap is reachable as one cohesive world.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmEscalian.Title: The Escalia World Map
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmEscalian.Content:
This shows how you can link releases together on a single world map.

You're looking at the Escalia World Map - the Beneos homebrew world where all our releases live.

You can download the Escalia World Map Pack for free via Moulinette if you want it for yourself.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmCityTiles.Title: Replaceable City Tiles
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmCityTiles.Content: The city names on this map are individual tiles. Want to make the world your own? Delete them and drop your own text fields on top.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmCampaign.Title: Build Your Own Campaign
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmCampaign.Content:
You can build an entire campaign on this principle: Country → Region → Place.

Yes, it's work. But if you want to do it, the Beneos documentation walks you through how to link releases together.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmContinents.Title: Switch Between Countries
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmContinents.Content:
On the full map, you can switch between countries by clicking their names. They're disabled in this tutorial.

What you see is just one of nine countries on this continent - and there are several continents in the world.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmDowercrag.Title: Castle Dourcrag
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmDowercrag.Content:
As an example, we placed Castle Dourcrag on the map. Hover the icon to see it belongs to Release 48 - the release number tells you which pack to download.

Dourcrag Castle alone has 24 maps in day and night versions - a huge environment reachable with a single click once installed.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmExample.Title: Just an Example
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmExample.Content:
This map is just an example to show you what's possible. You may never need to touch it.

But I didn't want to keep it from you - this is the exact map I built for my own players, and I wanted you to see what you can do with Beneos.
BENEOS.Tour.Page5.WmTutorialComplete.Con:
Congratulations! You've finished the Beneos Battle Maps tour.

Next: Beneos Tokens, Spells & Items - and how to bring your encounters to life with unique creatures, items and spell effects.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Creatures
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.Desc: Learn what makes Beneos creatures unique - from the search engine to the tactical guide and immersive integration.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtWelcome.Title: Beneos Creatures, Spells & Loot
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtWelcome.Content:
Welcome to Beneos Creatures, Spells & Loot - our second Patreon.

While Beneos Battlemaps delivers the environments, this Patreon fills them with 2.5D Tokens - professionally designed, playtested characters - plus items and spell effects. New content every month.

Let's walk through how to find, download and use these tokens - and what makes each one special.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtActorDirectory.Title: Open Beneos Cloud from the toolbar
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtActorDirectory.Conte:
Click the Beneos icon on the left toolbar to open Beneos Cloud - your gateway to creatures, items and spells.

Note: the toolbar appears once you have an active scene loaded. We're already on one, so the icon is right there waiting for you.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCloudOpen.Title: This Is the Beneos Cloud
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCloudOpen.Content:
The Beneos Cloud is the central hub for all Beneos downloads - Tokens, Items, Spells, and Maps.

Everything you download from us passes through this window.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCloudAccount.Title: A Beneos Cloud Account Is Required
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCloudAccount.Content:
To download Creatures, Spells or Items, you need a Beneos Cloud account.

Use the Sign in button at the bottom-left of the cloud window to log in or create one - the login dialog handles both. If you don't have an account yet, it will guide you through registration.

Important: use the same email address you have on Patreon. This auto-links your accounts and saves you from manual verification.

Note: only Beneos Tokens, Spells and Items require a Cloud account. Beneos Battlemaps still install through Moulinette.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCategories.Title: Find the Perfect Creature
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCategories.Content:
Content is organised into four categories: Tokens (creatures), Maps, Items and Spells.

We're on the Tokens tab now. Let's walk through the filters.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterBiome.Title: Filter by Biome
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterBiome.Content:
The Biome filter narrows creatures by environment - Forest, Desert, Cave, Swamp, Wasteland and more.

Perfect for assembling the right encounter for whatever region your players are in.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterCampaign.Title: Filter by Campaign
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterCampaign.Conte:
The Campaign filter surfaces creatures curated for popular adventures - Curse of Strahd, Shattered Obelisk and others.

A shortcut for dropping thematically perfect encounters into published modules.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterType.Title: Filter by Creature Type
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterType.Content:
The Creature Type filter supports main types and subtypes - for instance Humanoid → Humanoid Dwarf, or Fiend → Fiend Demon.

Makes it easy to build encounters with precise thematic cohesion.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterFaction.Title: Filter by Faction
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterFaction.Conten:
Every Beneos creature belongs to a Faction - a group of creatures that work well together, share traits and create powerful synergies in combat.

Pick creatures of the same faction to build encounters that feel like a coherent threat, not a random mob.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterFighting.Title: Filter by Fighting Style
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterFighting.Conte:
The Fighting Style filter describes how a creature fights: Ambush, Area of Effect, Lethal, Tanky, One-on-One, and more.

Build combats that play the way you want - from sneaky hit-and-run to pure attrition.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterPurpose.Title: Filter by Combat Purpose
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtFilterPurpose.Conten:
Purpose describes a creature's combat role: Anti-Melee, Anti-Caster, Lethal, Tank, Boss, Debuff, Gimmick and more.

Match the right roles to the right moments - your players will feel the difference.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSearchIntro.Title: Filter Broadly, Search Precisely
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSearchIntro.Content:
Filters help you explore and discover. If you already know what you want, use the search bar to find a creature by name.

Let's meet the Rot Cerf: ghastly to behold - towering, monstrous stag-beings with half-decayed visages, heralded by cracking leaves and the sweet stench of rot.

Not undead, but avatars of the eternal cycle of death, decay and rebirth. A single touch of their gnarled claws can infest a creature with unyielding decay - yet they're also capable of healing of mythical proportions.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSearchRotcerf.Title: Found the Rot Cerf
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSearchRotcerf.Conten:
We typed "Rot Cerf" into the search bar and the Cloud narrowed the results to the match.

The search works across name, description and metadata - partial names and related keywords all work.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSearchInstall.Title: The Install Button
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSearchInstall.Conten:
Every result has an Install button on the far right. Right now it reads Not Connected because we haven't signed into a Cloud account in this tutorial world.

Once signed in, a single click installs the creature. You can also drag & drop any result directly onto a scene.

For this tutorial we've pre-installed the Rot Cerf so we can show you what comes next.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtPlaceToken.Title: Installed and Standing on the Map
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtPlaceToken.Content:
Here's the Rot Cerf, installed and placed in the dungeon. Once a creature is installed, dropping it onto any scene is a single drag from the Actor sidebar.

Let's look at what makes a Beneos creature more than just a token on a grid.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtContextMenu.Title: Token HUD: Beneos Icons on Left & Right
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtContextMenu.Content:
This is what you see when you right-click a Beneos creature. On top of the standard Foundry Token HUD, Beneos adds two extra icons:

  • Journal icon (left) - opens the creature's Immersive Integration journal.
  • Skin icon (right) - switches between alternative appearances.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSkinSwitch.Title: Click the Skin Icon to Switch
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSkinSwitch.Content:
The skin icon on the right of the Token HUD switches the creature's appearance.

Click Next to see what happens.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSkinAlternate.Title: An Alternate Skin for Variety
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSkinAlternate.Conten:
The Rot Cerf just swapped to his alternate skin. Some Beneos tokens ship with up to 20 skins.

Three identical stag-beings on the same map suddenly look like three distinct threats - more visual texture without any extra setup.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSkinSwitchBack.Title: And Back to Skin 1
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtSkinSwitchBack.Conte: One click back. Stats, abilities and behaviour stay the same - only the visual changes.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtDeathIcon.Title: Automatic Death Icon at 0 HP
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtDeathIcon.Content:
When a Beneos creature drops to 0 HP, its token automatically switches to a death variant - an instant visual cue that the fight is over.

The Rot Cerf just went down. No configuration needed - this works on every Beneos creature.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtDeathIconSetting.Tit: You Can Disable the Automatic Death Icon
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtDeathIconSetting.Con:
This is an Avatar of Decay - a fitting look, but we can't leave him lying around forever. Back on his feet.

If you want to disable automatic death icons, toggle it in the Beneos module settings.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCharacterSheet.Title: Character Sheet: Fully Configured
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCharacterSheet.Conte:
Every Beneos creature ships with a fully configured character sheet - abilities, stats, custom icons and automation. Nothing for you to set up.

We update all creatures, items and spells regularly, so existing content keeps up with the latest Foundry features.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtDesignPhilosophy.Con:
Beneos creatures aren't just stat blocks. Each one is designed to tell a story through its actions - a meaningful role in your world and a distinctive way of fighting.

And we give you the tools to weave every creature into your own campaign. Stats are the beginning, not the end.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtBiographyTactical.Co:
On the Biography tab you'll find the Tactical Guide: an algorithmic breakdown of how the creature fights.

It's structured into consistent sections so you always know where to look - even when running a creature you've never seen before.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtTacticalDetail.Title: Guide Structure & Colour-Coding
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtTacticalDetail.Conte:
The Tactical Guide follows a consistent layout: General Traits, At the Start of Its Turn, Movement, Bonus Actions and Actions.

Shared concepts are colour-coded across creatures - the same colour always means the same thing. It may look dense at first, but it's designed so you can pick up any Beneos creature cold and run it confidently.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalOpen.Title: The Creature Journal
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalOpen.Content:
Every Beneos creature has a companion journal, accessible from a button at the bottom-left of the context menu.

This is where the real GM magic lives - beyond stats, you get tools to integrate this creature into your campaign.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalFullbody.Titl: Full Body Render at Full Size
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalFullbody.Cont:
The first page is a full-body render - a reference for you, and an image you can share with players at the moment of the reveal.

We've opened it as a full-size popout so you can see the detail. Every Beneos creature ships with art at this level.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalLore.Title: Immersive Integration: Lore
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalLore.Content:
The Immersive Integration page is the heart of the creature's narrative toolkit.

It starts with a Lore block - an atmospheric text that gives you the feel, tone and style of the creature. Read it once and you'll know how to present the Rot Cerf at your table.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalStory.Title: Story Prompts: Ready-Made Quests
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalStory.Content:
Next come Story Prompts - concrete quests built around this creature. Short, clear and packed with hooks that can fuel entire sessions.

Perfect for slotting a Beneos creature into your campaign as a focused arc, not a random encounter.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalForeshadowing:
Every creature leaves traces in the world. Foreshadowing Prompts list clues your players can find before the encounter - each with a skill check at a defined DC.

Succeed, and they learn something useful - sometimes even gaining a mechanical edge in the coming fight.
The Before Combat prompt is a cinematic read-aloud for the moment of the reveal - the instant initiative gets rolled.

Set the tone. All you have to do is read.
For each signature ability we provide a short in-combat read-aloud - a descriptive flourish that turns every ability into a moment, not a dice roll.

Finding the right words mid-combat is hard. We've done it for you.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalDeath.Title: Death Prompt: A Cinematic End
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtJournalDeath.Content: The Death Prompt - a read-aloud for the moment the creature falls. Give your players the cinematic closure they've earned.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtTokenRotation.Title: Token Rotation: A Foundry Default
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtTokenRotation.Conten:
When you move a token, Foundry automatically rotates it to face the direction it walked.

This is a Foundry VTT core feature, not a Beneos one. It works fine for top-down tokens, but Beneos creatures use a 2.5D perspective - rotating them looks unnatural. The good news: the setting can be turned off.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtRotationSetting.Cont:
Open Configure Settings → Core and find Automatic Token Rotation.

Uncheck it and Beneos tokens will keep their natural orientation no matter where they move.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtCreaturesComplete.Co:
This was the Rot Cerf - and every Beneos creature comes with the same depth: stats, tactical guide, lore, story prompts, foreshadowing, combat read-alouds and a death prompt.

Hundreds of creatures are ready for your table, with more every month.
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtLootSpellsNext.Title: Next: Loot & Spells
BENEOS.Tour.Page6.CtLootSpellsNext.Conte:
That's the full Tokens story. Next up: Beneos Loot & Spells.

This was by far the longest section - the rest is no less interesting, just more concise. Onward.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Loot
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.Desc: What makes Beneos magic items more than stat blocks: tiers, origins, set bonuses, and an honest pricing algorithm.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtWelcome.Title: Loot as Living Gameplay
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtWelcome.Content:
Beneos Loot isn't just a list of magic items. An item shouldn't only hand a player a bonus - it should create new gameplay.

Our items have upgrade mechanics, set bonuses, shop integration, and tools to weave them into your campaign. Let's walk through what makes a Beneos item special.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtCards.Title: Foundry-Ready and Printable
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtCards.Content:
Every Beneos item is fully integrated into Foundry VTT - but we also design each one as a printable card, available on Patreon and the Beneos Cloud.

Hand the cards to your players at the table. No more dictating stats or scribbling on notepad pages.

We're also gradually integrating the SRD items into this design, so there's no visual gap between Beneos items and the official D&D ones.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtTitle.Title: Item Title
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtTitle.Content: The top of every item card is the title - the name of the artifact your players are about to meet.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtLore.Title: Lore - Every Item Has a Story
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtLore.Content:
Every Beneos item has a lore text. Where it came from, who has wielded it, what it's known for.

The spark for your descriptions at the table - and the bridge between mechanics and narrative.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtFeatures.Title: Mechanical Features
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtFeatures.Content:
This block describes what the item does mechanically - active abilities, passive effects, charges, conditions.

All Foundry-ready and pre-configured.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtFooter.Title: Attunement & Tier
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtFooter.Content:
The footer shows whether the item requires attunement and its Tier - the power level:

  • Tier 1 - levels 1–5
  • Tier 2 - levels 5–10
  • Tier 3 - levels 10–15
  • Tier 4 - levels 15–20

Knowing the intended tier makes a huge difference when placing items in your game.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtTierUpgrade.Title: Tiers Are Gameplay, Not Just Labels
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtTierUpgrade.Content:
Tiers aren't just labels - they're a gameplay system. We provide ritual and quest templates that let players upgrade items between tiers.

A heirloom wielded at level 1 can grow all the way to level 20. Each upgrade adds new abilities and deepens the bond between character and item - turning a stat block into a story.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtPrice.Title: Honest Gold Pricing
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtPrice.Content:
Every Beneos item was priced by an internal algorithm that compares features across the entire catalogue.

Stronger items cost more, weaker items cost less - no random numbers. Drop any Beneos item into a magic shop and the price holds up.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtOrigin.Title: Item Origin - The Heart of Beneos Loot
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtOrigin.Content:
Every item has an Origin - a lore-and-mechanics root that says where it came from.

Vampire-forged. Dwarven-crafted. Divinely blessed. Born from biological mutation. We've designed 19 distinct origins, each with its own thematic identity.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtSetBonus.Title: Set Bonuses for Matching Origins
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtSetBonus.Content:
Players collecting items of the same Origin earn Set Bonuses.

One item = a strong item. Three items of the same Origin = thematic bonuses unique to that origin, plus the option to fuse them into a single ritual-bound artifact.

This motivates players to actively hunt for matching items.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtOriginSense.Title: Items That Pull You Into Adventure
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtOriginSense.Content:
Once a player attunes to an item of an Origin, they can sense other items of the same Origin in the surrounding miles.

On a journey from A to B, the party suddenly feels a Giant-Forged sword in a nearby tomb - and you have an unexpected dungeon crawl.

Loot becomes an adventure hook.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtFoundryView.Title: And This Is What It Looks Like in Foundry
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtFoundryView.Content:
Everything from the printable card is also fully integrated in Foundry VTT - title, lore, features, tier, attunement, price and origin, all in one sheet.

The Beneos mechanics (set bonuses, tier upgrades, origin sensing) hook into Foundry directly so you can run them at the table without paperwork.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtPdfIntro.Title: Every Item Comes With a PDF
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtPdfIntro.Content:
Every Beneos item, spell and creature ships with a printable PDF - downloadable from the Beneos Cloud.

Print them, share them digitally, or open them inside Foundry - your call.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtPdfPage6.Title: Read Up on the Origin Mechanics
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtPdfPage6.Content:
We've jumped to page 6 of the PDF - the full tier-upgrade and origin-set mechanics are documented here in detail.

Every Beneos PDF also ships in a printer-friendly black-and-white variant.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtItemFolder.Title: Browse the Beneos Items Folder
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtItemFolder.Content:
Every downloaded item and spell ends up in this folder in the Item Explorer.

We've added two more items here for you to browse at your own pace.
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtLootComplete.Title: Onward to Spells
BENEOS.Tour.Page7.LtLootComplete.Content:
That's Beneos Loot - items as gameplay engines, not just stat blocks.

Next: Beneos Spells, the final piece of the trio.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Spells
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.Desc: The short final chapter: what Beneos Spells are, what they are not, and how they plug into the rest of your game.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpWelcome.Title: Beneos Spells
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpWelcome.Content:
Welcome to the final chapter - Beneos Spells.

This is a shorter tour: a high-level look at what our spells are, what makes them different, and how to use them at your table.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpCard.Title: Not a Replacement - an Expansion
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpCard.Content:
Each spell card has a front (the full spell text) and a back (logo and name).

Beneos Spells don't replace existing spells. Every one of them has a specific goal: new ways to interact with the world or with enemies, and support for playstyles the standard list doesn't cover well.

They're carefully balanced for level, action type, use case and damage type. You won't find a 'better Fireball' here - you'll find spells that unlock gameplay the core list can't.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpFront.Title: The Front - the Spell Itself
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpFront.Content:
The front of the card is the spell: name, description, mechanics, dice, targets - everything a player needs to cast.

Designed for real table rhythm: pick it up, cast, put it back down.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpBack.Title: The Back - Identity at a Glance
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpBack.Content:
The back shows the logo and spell name.

Clean, distinct, stackable - perfect for a printed stack or for flipping through when someone asks "what was that one called?"
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpIcon.Title: Spell School & Level
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpIcon.Content:
This icon encodes two things: the spell's school (here: Evocation) and its level.

The more pips filled, the higher the level. This example has two filled pips - a 2nd-level Evocation spell.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpMonsters.Title: Spells in the Wild
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpMonsters.Content:
Many of these spells also live on Beneos Tokens. Our creatures often cast spells that don't exist in standard D&D - and your players can learn them by fighting the monsters that use them.

Imagine a campaign where wizards hunt monsters not just for loot, but for spells: defeat a creature, earn its signature spell. A great lever for pulling players into side quests or fights they'd otherwise walk past.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpFoundryView.Title: Evergreen by Design
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpFoundryView.Content:
Here's the same spell inside Foundry.

Just like our items, we keep every spell updated with each new Foundry VTT and D&D release, so they always work with the latest automation. You'll find the full catalogue in the spell folder in the Items sidebar.
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpComplete.Title: That's Beneos - Welcome Aboard
BENEOS.Tour.Page8.SpComplete.Content:
That closes out Spells - and the full Beneos tour.

Thank you for sticking with us. We hope you take as much joy in running these creatures, items and spells as we did designing them.

Happy adventuring.
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.Title: Beneos Tutorial - Your Next Steps
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.Desc: The wrap-up: where to go from here, what to grab, and who to talk to.
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnWelcome.Title: Your Turn
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnWelcome.Content:
That's the full tour - Battlemaps, Sceneries, Intros, World Map, Creatures, Loot, and Spells.

However polished the content is, you at the table turn it into a story. These tools are here to serve you - not the other way around. Now go build something your players will remember.
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnDungeon.Title: Start With a Dungeon
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnDungeon.Content:
Not sure where to start? Download the Beneos Mega Dungeon - or any of our maps - and see how they fit into your world.

Click this button to open Moulinette straight on the Mega Dungeon pack.
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnCloud.Title: Beneos Cloud
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnCloud.Content:
The Beneos Cloud is where your account lives - downloads, subscription, your personal library.

Click to open it in a new tab.
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnSearch.Title: Free Tokens, Items & Spells
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnSearch.Content:
The Beneos Search Engine browses everything we've published - including free tokens, items and spells - straight from inside Foundry.

Click to open it.
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnDiscord.Title: Come Say Hi
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnDiscord.Content:
Questions? Ideas? Want to share what your party just did with that Rot Cerf?

Our Discord is where we hang out with GMs and players who love this stuff. Click to join.
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnFarewell.Title: Go Tell a Great Story
BENEOS.Tour.Page9.FnFarewell.Content:
That's a wrap.

Let yourself be inspired. Impress your players.

- The Beneos Team
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.Title: Beneos Setup Tour
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.Desc: Guides you through first-time setup: Moulinette, authentication and content installation.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.WelcomeIntro.Title: Welcome to Beneos!
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.WelcomeIntro.Content:
Welcome! This tour will guide you step by step through the first-time setup of the Beneos Module.

You will learn how to connect with Moulinette, install content and set up your first scene.

You can restart this tour any time under Settings → Module Settings → Beneos.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouToolbar.Title: Moulinette - Your Content Manager
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouToolbar.Content:
Moulinette is the content manager you use to download and manage all Beneos content.

You can find it here in the left toolbar. It is the icon with the Moulinette logo.

Whenever you need to download new content, this is where you go.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouTools.Title: Moulinette Tools
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouTools.Content:
Clicking the Moulinette icon reveals its sub-tools in a second column:

  • Search (magnifying glass) - Opens the Moulinette Browser
  • User (person icon) - Opens User Authentication

The User icon is highlighted here. Let's use it to connect your Patreon account.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouAuthOpen.Title: User Authentication
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouAuthOpen.Content:
The User Authentication window is now open.

Here you connect your Patreon account so Moulinette knows you are a Beneos supporter and can provide you with all your content.

The Getting Started Tour is free - a free Patreon membership is enough to log in and download it. You do not need to be a paying supporter to try Beneos.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouAuthExplain.Title: Connect with Patreon
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouAuthExplain.Content:
Click "Sign in with Patreon" to link your Patreon account.

A new browser window will open for login. After successful connection your account details and active subscriptions will appear here.

Important: You must be logged in before continuing, otherwise the Moulinette Browser will not show your content.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouBrowserOpen.Title: Moulinette Browser
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouBrowserOpen.Content:
The Moulinette Browser is now open.

Here you find all available Beneos Battlemaps - fully configured scenes ready to download and use in your games.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouBrowserCreator.Cont:
To find Beneos content, look at the Packs section at the bottom of the filter panel on the left.

We have already selected "Beneos Battlemaps" as the Creator for you. All available Beneos packs are now shown.

In the future, select the creator from the Creator dropdown yourself.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouBrowserPack.Title: Selecting a Pack
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouBrowserPack.Content:
Now we have selected the - Beneos Getting Started Tour pack for you.

You can see the scenes from this pack in the results area. Each scene is a fully configured Beneos Battlemap.

Good to know: This Getting Started Tour is free for everyone - you don't need an active Patreon subscription to download it and explore what Beneos has to offer.

In the future, use the Pack dropdown to select any pack you want to install.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouInstallPack.Title: Importing a Pack
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouInstallPack.Content:
To install content, click on one of the maps and select "Import with Scene Packer".

We have selected "Start Here" for you. In the next step, the Scene Packer import dialog will open.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouScenepacker.Title: Scene Packer Import
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MouScenepacker.Content:
The Scene Packer import dialog is now open. You have two options:

  • Import All Entities in this Pack - Downloads the entire release with all scenes, tokens, items and sounds
  • Import Selected - Downloads only the single scene you selected

Click "Import All Entities" to download the complete pack and get started!
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.SetupComplete.Title: Setup Complete!
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.SetupComplete.Content:
Great job! You now know how to install Beneos content with Moulinette.

Next step: Install the Getting Started Pack using the steps you just learned, then start the Demo Tour to explore all features on a real scene.

You can find the Demo Tour under Settings → Module Settings → Beneos.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallConfirm.Title: Install Getting Started Tour?
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallConfirm.Content:
Would you like to install and immediately start the Getting Started Tour?

This is highly recommended for new Patrons or anyone curious about Beneos - everything is explained and demonstrated on real scenes, showing you exactly how to use it all.

If you choose Yes, we'll download the entire pack for you (as if you had clicked Import All yourself) and jump straight into the tutorial once it's ready.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MoulinetteLogin.Title: Patreon Login Required
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MoulinetteLogin.Conten:
It looks like you haven't logged into Moulinette with your Patreon account yet. Without a login, the Beneos pack cannot be downloaded.

Click Log in now - we'll open the Moulinette login window and wait here until you're logged in. The tour will continue automatically.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MoulinetteLogin.Waitin: After you finish the Patreon login, click I'm logged in below - Moulinette does not auto-refresh, so we'll reload its window to confirm.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.MoulinetteLogin.Fallba: We couldn't auto-confirm your Moulinette login. If you're sure you're logged in (e.g. you logged in outside Foundry), click Continue anyway to proceed.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallOverlay.Text: The Getting Started Tour is installing and will start automatically. Please wait…
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallOverlay.StillWo: Still installing - large packs can take a few minutes on slower connections. Please keep this window open…
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallError.Title: Beneos Setup - Installation Failed
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallError.NoButton: Couldn't find the Import-All button. Please run the Setup Tour again from the module settings.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallError.NoStart: Moulinette didn't start the installation. This usually means the Moulinette session timed out, you got signed out, or the network is unreachable. Check that you're still logged in to Moulinette and that your internet connection is working, then try again.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallError.Timeout: The installation took too long and was aborted. The Getting Started Tour pack should normally finish within a few minutes. Check your internet connection and try again - if it keeps timing out, please reach out on the Beneos Discord.
BENEOS.Tour.Setup.InstallError.Generic: The installation didn't complete. Try again, and if the problem persists, please reach out on the Beneos Discord.
BENEOS.Settings.AssetWatcher.Name: Check for missing Beneos assets
BENEOS.Settings.AssetWatcher.Hint: When enabled, the GM receives a popup on scene load if any Beneos-owned assets cannot be found on disk (e.g. after an interrupted download). Third-party assets are never checked.
BENEOS.AssetWatcher.DialogIntro: {count} Beneos asset(s) are missing on scene {scene}. This usually happens when a download was interrupted or files were removed from the Foundry data folder.
BENEOS.AssetWatcher.DialogHint: The troubleshooting journal walks you through reinstalling the missing content.
BENEOS.AssetWatcher.DialogReportAgain: Report this issue again next time the scene is loaded
BENEOS.AssetWatcher.PostInstall.MissingN: Beneos install check: {count} file(s) didn't land on disk after the Moulinette import on scene "{scene}". The scene may show 404 errors when opened.
BENEOS.AssetWatcher.PostInstall.AllOk: Beneos install check: all {count} files installed correctly.
BENEOS.AssetWatcher.PostInstall.DialogIn: The Moulinette import on scene {scene} finished, but {count} expected Beneos file(s) are missing on disk. This usually means the download was interrupted or one or more files transferred incompletely.
BENEOS.AssetWatcher.PostInstall.DialogHi: Re-running the install in Moulinette typically fixes this. Make sure no other downloads are competing for bandwidth, and that the Foundry data folder has enough free space.
BENEOS.Cloud.InstallVerify.DialogIntro: After installing {pack}, {missing} of {total} expected files aren't on disk. The scene will show broken assets when opened.
BENEOS.Cloud.InstallVerify.DialogGuidanc: Moulinette doesn't overwrite existing scenes or assets, so a partial install can't be auto-repaired. The reliable fix: in your Foundry world, delete this pack's scenes (or the entire pack folder), then re-install via Moulinette. If the problem repeats, please share the affected paths in the Beneos Discord support channel.
BENEOS.Cloud.InstallVerify.CopyConfirm: Copied to clipboard. Paste into the Beneos Discord support channel.

BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.001: The dice are watching. The dice are judging.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.002: A natural 1 builds character. A natural 20 builds legend.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.003: The d100 is just two dice in a trench coat.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.004: How do you want to do this?
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.008: "I have a bad feeling about this." - every Rogue on every door.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.009: Force persuasion is just Charisma with style.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.010: "One does not simply walk into the dungeon." - Give me a Stealth check.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.011: The wizard is never late. Nor early. He arrives precisely when initiative starts.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.012: You have my sword. And my bow. And my unprepared backstory.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.013: Second breakfast is a valid action economy choice.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.015: "I am no man." - every player exploiting RAW.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.016: "The North remembers." So does the DM.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.017: A Lannister always pays their debts. A player rarely does.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.019: You prepared three plot hooks. They picked a fourth one you invented mid-sentence.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.020: Every villain monologue: Interrupted on word two.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.021: Hunger dice are just regret dice with branding.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.022: The best riddles are the ones that never happen.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.026: The DM is smiling. That's never good.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.027: We've animated more torches than van Richten has swung.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.028: Chuck Norris reads Madame Eva's cards
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.029: Long rest in Barovia: 8 hours of sleep, 7 hours of nightmares, 0 hours of recovery.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.030: The dice know. The dice always know.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.031: Critical hits are earned. Critical fails are deserved.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.032: Your dice bag has more personality than your players character.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.033: Roll high. Roll often. Roll where the DM can see.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.034: A d20 is 5% hope and 95% consequences.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.035: 47 modules installed. 46 working. The hunt begins.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.037: "It worked last session." - a Online GM
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.038: Your world has 15 GB of assets. Beneos accounts for 11.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.039: The plot hook went unnoticed. The barmaid did not.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.040: "It's not a goth larp" - said the Vampire.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.041: Brujah solve problems. Tremere create Problems and Malkavians are the Problems.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.042: Roll Sanity. Roll it again. Yeah, that's what I thought.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.043: Cthulhu Investigators have two futures: doomed and slightly-less-doomed.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.044: Pathfinder 2e: for players who read the book. All of it. Twice.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.045: Three-action economy is just D&D admitting it had a problem.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.046: Vistani hospitality: charming, generous, deeply suspicious. Enjoy your stay.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.047: Long rest? In this dungeon? Bold.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.048: Yes, the wizard has a plan. No, you won't like it.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.049: Roll for initiative. Yes, all of you. Yes, even the bard.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.050: Hundreds of Beneos Original monsters. Why only use the same six?
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.051: 80% of projects are solved in just one Fireball.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.052: Hi, I'm Beneos Cloud. I'm cool.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.053: Yes, there's a new map. There's always a new map.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.054: Static maps are fine. So is black-and-white television.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.055: A still map is just a video buffering.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.056: Compatible with Foundry. Hostile to mediocrity.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.057: Roll20 called. They want their 2014 UI back.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.058: One release at a time, since 2021. With love and too much coffee.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.059: Half of GMing is the eyebrow.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.060: Pen, paper, and an irresponsible amount of plastic dice.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.061: Originally a wargame. Now mostly improv theater with math.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.062: Reminder: feed your players. Snacks. Plot. Both.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.063: Don't forget to give your players Heroic Inspiration.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.064: Static maps are just animated maps loading slowly.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.065: The most important skill for a DM is knowing what kind of DM they are and what types of players are in their group.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.066: Beneos creatures abilities always tell a story in battle.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.067: We have so much stuff, we need our own search engine.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.068: Beneos: making your D&D sessions gloriously GPU-bound.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.070: If something breaks, it's probably a different module. Of course, it's never our fault. never...
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.071: Foundry: now with 12% more module conflicts.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.072: Compatibility with the latest Foundry version: confident-ish.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.074: Strahd respects a good fight. He respects ambushes more.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.075: The dice gods are listening. They are also laughing.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.076: How funny the world would be if you had a 5% chance of completely screwing things up every time you did something.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.079: The bard is, regrettably, attempting to seduce it again.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.080: Of course the fighter has a 7 in Intelligence. He's a fighter.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.081: Have you tried setting it on fire?
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.082: Lawful Good is not a personality.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.083: Murder hobos welcome. Tip your barbarian.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.084: Nat 20 on Persuasion IS NOT mind control.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.085: The session zero matters. You skipped session zero, didn't you.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.086: The DM is rolling behind the screen for "narrative reasons".
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.087: Behind the screen: Wikipedia, vibes, and prayer.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.088: Insight is not a lie detector.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.089: Don't worry, that isn't supposed to kill you. Probably.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.090: Yes, the innkeeper has a name… now.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.091: Improvise. Adapt. Pretend you planned this.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.092: The wizard tower was, in fact, suspiciously poorly defended.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.093: You're prepping for tomorrow's session, aren't you.
BENEOS.Cloud.Quotes.094: Rules as Written vs. Rules as Intended vs. Rules as Fun: pick two.
